framework.source: inferred: @sveltejs/kit → sveltekit (package is the SvelteKit framework itself)

examples.note: Dynamic segment [slug] is accessible via params.slug in load functions and components.
Optional segment [[lang]] makes the prefix optional; both /home and /en/home resolve to this route.
Same file handles both /home and /en/home due to the optional segment.
Rest segment captures multiple path parts into a single params.rest string.
Layout at src/routes/settings/+layout.svelte is inherited by all child routes under /settings/.
Route group (app) is purely organizational — parentheses are stripped from the URL.
Parameter matcher [page=fruit] validates the segment against src/params/fruit.js before matching.

hooks.name: beforeNavigate
hooks.access: Must be called during Svelte component initialization (imported from $app/navigation)

availableApi.name: navigation.from
availableApi.description: Route information for the origin of the navigation (url, params, route.id).

availableApi.name: navigation.to
availableApi.description: Route information for the navigation destination (url, params, route.id). Null for non-SvelteKit routes.

availableApi.name: navigation.willUnload
availableApi.description: Boolean — true when the document will fully unload (e.g. navigating to an external URL).

availableApi.name: navigation.cancel()
availableApi.description: Call to prevent the navigation. For 'leave' navigations, triggers the browser's native unload dialog.

hooks.name: onNavigate
hooks.access: Must be called during Svelte component initialization (imported from $app/navigation)

availableApi.name: navigation.from
availableApi.description: Route information for the origin of the navigation.

availableApi.name: navigation.to
availableApi.description: Route information for the navigation destination.

availableApi.name: callback return value
availableApi.description: Optionally return a function; SvelteKit calls it after DOM updates complete. Used for view transitions via document.startViewTransition.

hooks.name: afterNavigate
hooks.access: Must be called during Svelte component initialization (imported from $app/navigation). Also fires on component mount.

availableApi.name: navigation.from
availableApi.description: Route information for the origin of the navigation. Null on initial page load.

availableApi.name: navigation.to
availableApi.description: Route information for the completed navigation destination.

hooks.name: navigating (store/$app/state)
hooks.access: Import navigating from '$app/state' (Svelte 5 / SvelteKit 2.12+) or '$app/stores' (legacy). Reactive — subscribe or use with $derived.

availableApi.name: navigating.from
availableApi.description: Origin route of the in-progress navigation.

availableApi.name: navigating.to
availableApi.description: Destination route of the in-progress navigation.

hooks.name: reroute (universal hook)
hooks.access: Defined in src/hooks.js (or src/hooks.ts) as an exported reroute function. Not a component-level hook.

availableApi.name: url
availableApi.description: The URL object for the incoming request. The hook returns a pathname string to redirect to a different route without changing the browser address bar.

routingChanges: resolvePath removed in favour of resolveRoute from $app/paths
depends() now requires a colon as part of the string
Top-level promise awaiting in load functions no longer supported
goto() can no longer navigate to external URLs
$app/state module introduced as replacement for $app/stores, providing page, navigating, and updated reactive state objects with Svelte 5 runes support
reroute hook now supports async operations including data fetching

structured_output.notes: SvelteKit uses filesystem-based routing exclusively — there is no config-object style. Routes live under src/routes/. Special files use a '+' prefix: +page.svelte, +layout.svelte, +server.js, etc. Route groups use (groupName) directory syntax and have no effect on URLs. Parameter matchers in src/params/ can constrain dynamic segments. The router.type config option ('pathname' | 'hash') selects the history strategy; hash mode disables SSR and prerendering. $app/stores is deprecated in SvelteKit 2.12+ in favour of $app/state (Svelte 5 runes). The onNavigate hook supports returning a callback that fires post-DOM-update, making it suitable for View Transitions API integration. afterNavigate fires on the initial component mount as well as subsequent navigations.
